original: The Mars Odyssey finds signs of large ice deposits on the planet Mars
J. K. Rowlingbegins publishing children's story "The Ickabog" online in installments
William and Frederick Langenheim take eight sequential photographs of the first total eclipse of the sun visible in North America since the invention of photography
Apollo 10 astronauts return to Earth
George Willig climbs the South Tower of NYC's World Trade Center, famously fined 1 cent for each of 110 stories he climbed
Only three days after a previous record, Sherpa Lakpa Gelu climbs Mount Everest in 10 hours 56 minutes. The tourism ministry of Nepal confirms this record in July that year.
A super "blood" moon, the first total lunar eclipse for two years, visible across the Pacific
Hwang Dong-hyuk, South Korean filmmaker (directed Squid Game), born in Seoul, South Korea
Matt Stone, American actor, animator and producer (South Park, The Book of Mormon), born in Houston, Texas
Raina Telgemeier, American cartoonist (Smile), born in San Francisco, California
HMS Challenger returns from 128,000-km oceanographic exploration
USAF bomber flies Atlantic in a record of just over 3 hours
Lewis and Clarkfirst sight the Rocky Mountains
Soyuz 36 carries cosmonautsValery Kubasovand the first Hungarian in space, Bertalan Farkas, to Salyut 6
In Major League Baseball, Carlos Martinez famously hits a ball off JoseCanseco's head for a home run

Premiere of "AbbaVoyage", a virtual concert with computer generated images of the Swedish pop band and live musicians at the specially built 3,000 seat Abba Arena in London, England; all four members, as well as the king and queen of Sweden attend
Muhammed Faris, first Syrian astronaut in space (Soyuz TM-3), born in Aleppo, Syria (d. 1951)
NASA launches Exosat
Joe FrazierTKOs Ron Stander in 5 for heavyweight boxing title
Lifts for the recently opened Eiffel Tower are ready, offering an option other than 1,710 steps, for visitors to get to top
MLB Pittsburgh Pirates Harvey Haddix pitches 12 perfect innings, loses in 13th to Braves at Milwaukee County Stadium
Freedom Ride Coordinating Committee establishes in Atlanta
Russia reports it has started vaccinating domestic animals against COVID-19, using the Karnivak-Kov vaccine
"Dracula" by Irish authorBram Stokeris published by Archibald Constable and Company in London
EMI rush releases The Beatles' album "Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band" in London and select markets in the UK, goes to #1 for 22 weeks in the UK and 15 weeks in the US
Monkey Punch [Kazuhiko Kato], Japanese manga cartoonist (Lupin III), born in Hamanaka, Hokkaido, Empire of Japan (d. 2019)
Neshka Robeva, Bulgarian rhythmic gymnast (World C'ship 3 x silver, 1 x bronze 1969) and coach (World, European and Olympic competition, 294 medals), born in Rousse, Bulgaria
Nobuhiro Watsuki, Japanese manga artist (Rurouni Kenshin), born in Tokyo, Japan
Mysterious feral child Kaspar Hauser is discovered wandering the streets of Nuremberg
German Emanuel Lasker becomes undisputed World Chess Champion by beating Austrian-American defending titleholder Wilhelm Steinitz, 10-5 (4 draws) in Montreal, Canada

Mr. and Mrs. Jacob Murdock and their children become the first family to travel across the United States by car, driving from Los Angeles to New York City in a Packard Thirty in 32 days, 5 hours, and 25 minutes
Tennis game in Surrey championships lasts 31 minutes
Stanley Cup Final, Northlands Coliseum, Edmonton, AL: Edmonton Oilers beat Boston Bruins, 6-3 for 4 game series sweep
Future Baseball Hall of Fame center fielderTy Cobbis first to collect 1,000 extra-base hits when he doubles in Detroit Tigers' 8-1 win against the Chicago White Sox at Comiskey Park
Dorothea Lange, American documentary photographer, born in Hoboken, New Jersey (d. 1965)
Moondog [Louis Thomas Hardin], American composer, musician and poet, born in Marysville, Kansas (d. 1999)
J Hus [Momodou Lamin Jallow], English rapper and singer (Beautiful and Brutal Yard), born in London, England
Henry Fordand the Ford Motor Company produce the last (and 15 millionth) Model T Ford / Tin Lizzie
The Soviet Tupolev Tu-144 becomes the first commercial transport to exceed Mach 2
"Funky Worm" by Ohio Players hits #15
Space Shuttle Challenger moves to launch pad for STS-7
Super Rugby Final, Canberra: Fullback Andrew Walker kicks 5 penalties & 3 conversions as the ACT Brumbies win their first title; beat Coastal Sharks (Durban), 36-6
Cleveland Cavaliers win the NBA Eastern Conference
Ken Wyatt becomes Australia's first Aboriginal minister in government as the minister for indigenous Australians
Antonia Forest [Patricia Giulia Caulfield Kate Rubinstein], British children's author (The Marlows), born in Hampstead, London (d. 2003)
Levon Helm, American drummer and singer (The Band - "Up On Cripple Creek"; "The Weight"), and actor (Coal Miner's Daughter; The Right Stuff), born in Elaine, Arkansas (d. 2012)
Erica Terpstra, Dutch swimmer (Olympic silver 4×100m medley relay, bronze 4×100m freestyle relay 1964) and politician (VVD), born in The Hague, Netherlands
Garry Peterson, Canadian drummer (Guess Who - "American Woman"; "No Sugar Tonight"), born in Winnipeg, Manitoba
Masaharu Morimoto, Japanese chef and TV personality (Iron Chef, Iron Chef America), born in Hiroshima, Japan
Isaac Slade, American vocalist, pianist, and songwriter (The Fray, 2002-22 - "How To Save A Life"; "You Found Me"), born in Boulder, Colorado
Zećira Mušović, Swedish football goalkeeper (Chelsea, Sweden), born in Falun, Sweden
Battle of Avarayr: Armenian rebels are defeated militarily by the Sassanid Empire but are guaranteed freedom to openly practice Christianity
Moon Jaguar becomes the 10th ruler of the Maya city of Copán (modern Honduras), will reign for 25 years
Emily Duncan becomes Great Britain's first woman magistrate
Armenia defeats the Ottoman Army in the Battle of Sardarapat
Organization of African Unity forms
Guyana (formerly British Guiana) declares independence from the United Kingdom
Beatles' "Beatles 1967-1970" album (the blue one) goes #1
The United States Supreme Court rules that Ellis Island, the historic gateway for millions of immigrants, is mainly in the state of New Jersey, not New York
Napoleon Bonaparteis crowned King of Italy
